stories.title: Hungary’s Magyar heading to Brussels for talks on unlocking EU funds
stories.summary: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar will travel to Brussels on Wednesday for urgent talks with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen as he races to unfreeze billions in EU funds. “I will travel to Brussels on Wednesday for informal talks with the president of the European Commission on unlocking EU funds,” Magyar wrote on X on Sunday. “We have no time to waste.” A delegation from Magyar’s Tisza party, which won a supermajority in Hungary’s April 12 election, m

stories.title: Orbán associates rush to move wealth out of Hungary after election defeat
stories.summary: Incoming PM Péter Magyar accuses Fidesz-linked figures of trying to shield their wealth from accountability Along the banks of the Danube, news that the Viktor Orbán era had come to an end set off an hours-long party. The joy echoed across Hungary as people traded hugs and high-fives. For some, however, the landslide loss set off a frantic scramble. Private jets allegedly laden with the spoils of those whose wealth swelled during Orbán’s 16 years in power have steadily been taking off from Vienn

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy signs agreement with Azerbaijan as death toll from Russian attacks rises to 10
stories.summary: Ukrainian leader said agreement related to military-industrial co-operation; Russia pounds city of Dnipro with missiles and drones. What we know on day 1,523 Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed deals on security and energy cooperation with his Azerbaijani counterpart Ilham Aliyev in Baku on Saturday, he said, as Kyiv seeks to leverage its experience in defending its airspace from Russia. After the latest wave of conflict in the Middle East that began with US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran

stories.title: Beijing lashes out at EU after Chinese firms included in latest Russia sanctions
stories.summary: Beijing warned Brussels that the “EU will bear all consequences” after the bloc included Chinese companies in its latest sanctions package against Russia, escalating tensions in an already strained Sino-Europe trade relationship. In a statement issued late Saturday, China’s commerce ministry said it was “strongly dissatisfied” and “firmly opposes” the inclusion of Chinese businesses in the Russia sanctions, accusing the EU of acting “brazenly” despite repeated objections. “China urges the

stories.title: A HUN-REN vezetői: Tragédia lenne a magyar tudománynak, ha visszacsatolnák az MTA-hoz a kutatóintézeteket
stories.summary: Gulyás Balázs elnök és Jakab Roland vezérigazgató nem aggódik a Tisza kétharmada miatt, szerintük a kutatói hálózat nyugat-európai mintára működik, egy Európa-központú kormányzat pedig nem vinné keletre a magyar tudományt.

stories.title: Az EU-s pénzek és Vitézy kinevezése sokat lendíthet a fővárosi fejlesztéseken
stories.summary: A budapesti villamoshálózat fejlesztése valószínűleg fel fog gyorsulni a következő kormányzati ciklusban, a Galvani híd megvalósulásának ugyanakkor nem mindenki örülne.

stories.title: Kósa Lajos sem ül be a Parlamentbe, 36 év után búcsúzik
stories.summary: Ezt a képviselő Facebook-oldalán jelentette be. A Fidesz tisztújításán sem vesz részt.

stories.title: Már csak a magyar félen múlik a Budapest-Belgrád megnyitása a szerb közlekedési miniszter szerint
stories.summary: Szerbia készen áll, a magyar oldalon van hiba, amit a MÁV ellenőriz, mondta a szerb miniszter.

stories.title: A Michael Jackson életéről szóló film rekordbevételt hozott össze az első hétvégén
stories.summary: Már a magyar mozikban is vetítik.

stories.title: Magyar Péter világosan megmutatta, hogy beleáll abba a konfrontációba, amit Orbán nagyon próbált kikerülni
stories.summary: Újraosztják a lapokat a magyar–szlovák viszonyban, Magyar pedig jelezte, nem fogja annyiban hagyni a Beneš-dekrétumok ügyét. A szlovák kormány viszont láthatóan kész tovább élezni a kialakult helyzetet, és egy millimétert sem készülnek hátrálni. A jövő évi szlovák választás előtt Ficóék rá is játszhatnak a magyarellenességre.

stories.title: A reptéren vették őrizetbe a szegedi Fidesz-elnököt, aki azután mondott le posztjáról, hogy kiderült: körözik
stories.summary: Az RTL információi szerint péntek este fogta el az adóhatóság a férfit a budapesti reptéren.

stories.title: Az atomkatasztrófát viszonylag könnyen megúsztuk, de a magyar társadalomban nyomot hagyott Csernobil
stories.summary: A hazai szakemberek reakcióit és intézkedéseit utólag igazolta az idő, de a trauma, ami ért minket, közös. Bár Magyarországon nem volt jelentős szennyezés, az információhiány miatt az emberek mentális egészségét megviselte az az időszak. Az MTA Csernobil 40 konferenciáján jártunk.

stories.title: Guardian: Sietve menekíti Orbán Viktor köre Amerikába és más országokba a vagyonát
stories.summary: A lap arról ír, hogy Bécsből folyamatosan szállnak fel a magángépek.

stories.title: Hanged under the cover of war: letters and videos tell stories of Iran’s death row victims
stories.summary: Testimony emerges from Babak Alipour, who spent three years on death row before being taken to gallows in March Writing from his cell in the Rajai Shahr prison in the northern Iranian city of Karaj, Babak Alipour wanted to tell his friends about those who had already gone to their execution. There was Behrouz Ehsani, 69, the elder statesman of the group, who was “never angry” about their predicament. Then there was Mehdi Hassani, a 48-year-old father of three who he saw a couple of times in the

stories.title: Lövések dördültek a Fehér Házi Tudósítók Vacsorájának helyszínén, Trumpot és feleségét ki kellett menekíteni
stories.summary: Egy rendőrre rálőttek a washingtoni szállodában tartott protokolleseményen, ahova több mint 2500 vendég volt hivatalos.

stories.title: Mélissa Da Costa: Visszatérés (részlet)
stories.summary: Ambre húszéves, és teljesen Philippe-hez, a negyvenéves, nős, kétgyermekes férfihoz kötődik, aki szeretőként uralja az életét. Amikor a kétségbeesés elhatalmasodik rajta és összeomlik, Philippe egy hegyi hotelbe küldi, hogy távol tőle újra felépülhessen. Ambre hat hónapot tölt ott szezonmunkások között, és lassan megtanul kilépni a függőségből, visszatalálni önmagához, megnyílni másoknak – és újra hinni a szeretetben. Olvasson bele a nagy sikerű Az ég minden kékje – tavaly a legnagyobb példánysz

stories.title: Az amerikai neonáci, aki öngyilkos lett, miután leleplezte zsidó származását a New York Times
stories.summary: Dan Burros 28 éves koráig tagja volt az Amerikai Náci Pártnak, de a Ku-Klux-Klannak is prominens alakja lett, a gyökereit azonban nem tudta a végtelenségig titkolni.

stories.title: A székely Lizike nem akart politizálni, a végén mégis a miniszterekkel szelfiző propagandista lett
stories.summary: A kampányidőszakban Erdélyben is feltűnően megszaporodtak azok az influenszerek, akik nyíltan a Fidesz mellett kezdtek el kampányolni: közülük két látványosabb szereplő György Hajnalka, vagyis Lizike, valamint Veres Zsolt volt. A Transtelex már jóval korábban írt Lizikéről, még akkor, amikor a Celeste Barber által népszerűvé tett paródiákat „székelyesítve” futott be, és ezekkel épített komoly követőtábort. Innen jutott el odáig, hogy a választási kampányban már politikai megmondóként, a Fidesz ü

stories.title: Abszolút filmszínház: Magyar Péter filmfesztiválra utazik Olaszországba május elején
stories.summary: A Riviera Nemzetközi Filmfesztiválon a Tavaszi szél című dokumentumfilmet is bemutatják.

stories.title: Amatőr videójáték-fejlesztő és mesterdiplomás tanár a Trump elleni lövöldözéssel gyanúsított férfi
stories.summary: Egy keresztény hallgatói közösségben és egy Nerf-fegyverekkel harcoló campus-csoportban is részt vett korábban, volt a hónap tanára, és 25 dollárral támogatta Kamala Harris kampányát.

stories.title: Most bárki megnézheti, hogy milyen a világon egyedülálló budapesti előadás
stories.summary: Ma már előadásonként 15-20 külföldi néző csak azért utazik Budapestre, hogy lássa a Walk My World produkciót.

stories.title: A romániai orosz nagykövet nem cáfolta kategorikusan, hogy a szombaton Galacra zuhanó drón orosz eredetű volt
stories.summary: Oana Ţoiu külügyminiszter közölte, hogy mi volt a nagykövet reakciója az incidens után.

stories.title: Lakner szerint azzal, hogy Orbán nem ül be a parlamentbe, megint azokat csapja be, akik rá szavaztak
stories.summary: Azokat, „akiknek azt ígérte még, hogy még vereség esetén is a parlamentben képviseli őket” – reagált a politikai elemző.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerdán Ursula von der Leyennel tárgyal az uniós pénzek hazahozataláról
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök szerint nincs vesztegetni való idő.

stories.title: A BYD kellett hozzá: csak évekkel a gyár érkezése után kezdhetik felújítani a Szeged felé vezető vasutat
stories.summary: Több mint egy évtizede napirenden van, de jobb esetben is csak 2027-ben kezdődhet el a Szeged–Kiskunfélegyháza-vasútvonal felújítása. A megvalósítás így a Tisza-kormányra hárul.

stories.title: Orbán is üzent Trumpnak az éjjeli lövöldözés után
stories.summary: Sorra reagáltak az európai vezetők a történtekre: Emmanuel Macron elfogadhatatlannak nevezte a fegyveres támadást, Giorgia Meloni szerint „politikai gyűlöletnek nincs helye demokráciáinkban”.

stories.title: Szijjártó Péter adriai jachtozásról szóló története még a korábbinál is ordítóbb kérdéseket vet fel
stories.summary: Nem Szíjj László NER-es oligarcha, hanem a legjobb barátja, Benkő Szilárd meghívására nyaralt az adriai jachton Szijjártó Péter leköszönő külügyminiszter 2020-ban. Ezzel viszont nem kerültünk közelebb a kételyek lezárásához.

stories.title: Nagyzolás és túlzás lenne, ha világbajnokként mutatkoznék be
stories.summary: Horváth Laura 2023-ban a világ legfittebb nője lett, miután megnyert egy amerikai versenyt. 2025-ben is bajnok volt, most pedig dokumentumfilmet készítettek róla.

stories.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.summary: Languorous tree dwellers from Guyana and Peru died from ‘cold stun’ in warehouse with no power or running water Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Wildlife officials in Florida said in a newly released report that dozens of sloths taken from South American rainforests for display at a controversial new tourist attraction in Orlando died in the care of their new owners. stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strike on Odesa kills 2, hits hospitals and schools overnight
stories.summary: Ukraine said Russian forces struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa overnight on 23 to 24 April 2026, killing at least two people and injuring more than a dozen.

stories.title: Az orbáni oligarchák tízmilliárdos értékben utalják ki a pénzt távoli országokba Magyar Péter szerint
stories.summary: A megválasztott miniszterelnök úgy tudja, több „oligarcha család” is elhagyta már az országot, Mészáros Lőrincék pedig hamarosan Dubajba repülnek.

stories.title: Tizennyolc év az örökkévalóság
stories.summary: Két évvel ezelőtt a Telex fotósa egy balesetben elvesztette az öccsét. A tragédia utóhatásáról készült sorozat André Kertész Nagydíjat nyert az idei magyar sajtófotók között. Melegh Noémi Napsugár sorozata traumáról, családról és arról az utolsó gimnáziumi évről, ahol életre szóló élményeket kellene gyűjteni, de csak a gyász és a végtelen szomorúság marad.

stories.title: Magyar Péter arról beszélt, hogy árulják a „rogáni gyűlöletpropaganda anyahajóját”, a cég szerint nincs értékesítés alatt a Lounge Event Kft.
stories.summary: „Ilyen irányú folyamat jelenleg nincs a cégcsoportban, a megjelent információk valótlanok” – válaszolta kérdésünkre a Lounge Group.

stories.title: Benedek Szabolcs: Ha Nyugaton születik, világsztár lett volna
stories.summary: „Fel akartam hát nőni minél hamarabb, csak azt az egy apróságot kellett még hozzá kitalálnom, hogy mi váljék belőlem.” E heti novellánk a gyerekkori álmokról és azok valóra váltásáról szól.

stories.title: Tényleg végzetesen lemaradt Európa, vagy csak nem veszünk észre valami fontosat?
stories.summary: Nincs európai Google, de az is lényeges, hogy hány magyar, toszkán, breton, bajor kkv-nak van stabil nete és stabil megrendelése. Mert lehet, hogy ott van az igazi versenyképesség.

stories.title: Europe is in a profound state of crisis. Luckily, we know what to do | Nathalie Tocci and Anu Bradford
stories.summary: We assembled a group of the continent’s leading thinkers to assess the threats: their warnings are stark, but the remedy is within reach Caught between Vladimir Putin’s Russia, Donald Trump’s US and Xi Jinping’s China, Europe appears in a state of profound crisis, the narrative about its future often filled with fatalism. There is a paradox, however. Despite rising nationalism, the climate crisis and the economic slowdown , few would take issue with the claim that Europe still has a great deal g

stories.title: US millionaire big-game hunter dies after being crushed by elephants
stories.summary: Ernie Dosio, a 75-year-old vineyard owner, was hunting an antelope species in Africa when the incident occured Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox An American millionaire big-game hunter has died after being crushed by a group of elephants during a hunting expedition in Gabon. Ernie Dosio, a 75-year-old vineyard owner, was hunting yellow-backed duiker, an antelope species, in the central African country of Gabon when the incident occurred last Friday. Wh

stories.title: Altman apologizes after OpenAI failed to alert police before fatal Canada shooting
stories.summary: OpenAI said the company had identified an account using abuse-detection efforts, but determined at the time it didn’t meet threshold for legal referral The head of OpenAI has written a letter apologizing that his company didn’t alert law enforcement about the online behavior of a person who shot and killed eight people in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia. In the letter posted on Friday, Sam Altman expressed his deepest condolences to the entire community. Continue reading...

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: At first summit without Orbán, Zelenskyy rejects “symbolic” EU membership
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU approves €90 billion loan for Ukraine after Hungary lifts controversial veto.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Kim Jong-un strengthens military ties with Russia and hails soldiers who fought in Kursk
stories.summary: Russian delegation visits Pyongyang; 16 killed in drone strikes across region; Zelenskyy accuses Putin of ‘nuclear terrorism’ over Chornobyl risks. What we know on day 1,524 North Korean leader Kim Jong-un has hailed the troops from his country who fought alongside Russia in Kursk a year ago , state media KCNA said on Monday. Kim made his remarks after a Russian delegation arrived in Pyongyang to attend the opening ceremony of a memorial complex honouring those killed helping Moscow’s invasion o

stories.title: Iran’s foreign minister back in Pakistan, but uncertainty clouds peace talks
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi returned to Islamabad on Sunday night, rejoining mediation efforts just hours after he left Pakistan and U.S. President Donald Trump abruptly scrapped a planned trip by American envoys to revive peace talks with Tehran. Iranian state media confirmed Araghchi’s return on Sunday, reporting he had arrived back in the Pakistani capital “to continue consultations on ending the war with the United States” after a brief stop in Oman. The minister had left Islamaba

stories.title: Pro-Starmer think tank Labour Together plans major overhaul after scandal
stories.summary: LONDON — The think tank credited with helping to deliver Keir Starmer’s 2024 election landslide is set to overhaul its operations and change its name after being rocked by scandal. In her first public interview since being appointed as chief executive of Labour Together last September, Alison Phillips told POLITICO a full rebrand of the organization will take place in mid-May as it attempts to tackle perceptions of it being a “factional boys’ club.” “That’s hu

stories.title: What Britain wants from King Charles’ trip to Trumpland
stories.summary: LONDON — When King Charles III lands in the U.S. on Monday, his aides will be carrying a heavy ring binder lined with thick, textured paper. Among reams of timings, briefings and biographies will likely be a condensed list of key objectives, the result of months of planning with the British government, expanded upon in the margins with handwritten scribbles from the monarch’s red felt-tip pen. It will not have been easy to write. Trump had already strained his relationship with British Prime Min

stories.title: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes kill 14 in deadliest day since ceasefire began
stories.summary: The Israeli government and Hezbollah have traded blame over breaches to the truce, which is set to run for several more weeks Lebanon’s health ministry said Israeli strikes on the country’s south killed 14 people on Sunday, the deadliest day since a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah came into force over a week ago. The health ministry said the dead on Sunday included two women and two children, adding that 37 other people were wounded. Israel said one of its soldiers was also killed. Contin

stories.title: Anti-Trump sentiment being examined as motive for White House press dinner shooting
stories.summary: Manifesto reportedly written by the suspect had Trump administration officials at top of list Trump tells 60 Minutes he ‘wasn’t worried’ during shooting How the White House press dinner shooting unfolded Investigators are looking into anti-Trump sentiment as being a motive for the attacker who sought to breach the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington DC where the US president and top members of his administration were present. Officials have said that the shooter likely was targeting

stories.title: King Charles to proceed with US trip despite security concerns
stories.summary: Buckingham Palace said King Charles III’s state visit to the U.S. this week will proceed as planned despite security concerns raised by Saturday’s shooting in Washington at a dinner attended by U.S. President Donald Trump. “Following discussions on both sides of the Atlantic through the day, and acting on advice of Government, we can confirm the State Visit by Their Majesties will proceed as planned,” the Palace said in a statement Sunday evening. King Charles and Queen C

stories.title: Time for a different kind of NATO
stories.summary: Ivo Daalder, a former U.S. ambassador to NATO, is a senior fellow at Harvard University’s Belfer Center and host of the weekly podcast “World Review with Ivo Daalder.” He writes POLITICO’s From Across the Pond column . Can NATO be saved? I’ve been asked this question with increasing frequency since U.S. President Donald Trump began repeatedly denigrating the alliance, and then threatening to withdraw from it altogether. My answer? Yes. But not the same NATO we’ve come to know over the last 77 ye

stories.title: German conservatives pile pressure on von der Leyen to dismantle Brussels ‘machine’
stories.summary: BERLIN — German conservatives are preparing to confront European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen with a stark ultimatum: Rein in Brussels control and red tape or face a new push to curb the Commission’s powers. Von der Leyen is set to attend a gathering of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s conservatives in Berlin on Monday, where the group plans to face her with tougher demands on fast-tracking cuts to what they see as burdensome EU regulations weighing on German businesses, two of th

stories.title: US officials trying to figure out how suspect got gun into Trump venue
stories.summary: Law enforcement are investigating how a suspect got a firearm into the venue where the White House Correspondents’ Dinner was held Saturday, acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said. The suspect — who was taken into custody Saturday night  after a shooting  that rattled lawmakers, administration officials and reporters — is not cooperating with law enforcement, he said. Neither President Donald Trump nor any other attendees of the dinner were harmed during the shooting, though a Secre

stories.title: Suspected gunman at White House press dinner named
stories.summary: Law enforcement agencies name Cole Tomas Allen as Trump posts video of man sprinting through checkpoint The suspected gunman in the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner that has roiled Washington has been identified as Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old man from southern California. Allen, of Torrance, a suburb of Los Angeles, has no record of criminal charges or a civil court history in Los Angeles county, according to a records search. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mali defence minister killed amid flurry of insurgent attacks
stories.summary: Car bomb kills Sadio Camara at home during coordinated assaults by rebel groups including West African al-Qaida affiliate Mali’s defence minister was killed in an attack on his residence, the government said on Sunday, a high-profile fatality during coordinated assaults staged the previous day by insurgents including the West African affiliate of al-Qaida. A car laden with explosives driven by a suicide attacker drove into Sadio Camara’s residence in the town of Kati, the spokesperson, Issa Ousm

stories.title: Renewable energy will boost national security and protect UK from sabotage, minister says
stories.summary: Widely dispersed wind farms and solar panels are harder to target than fossil fuel power stations, Michael Shanks says Renewable energy will boost the UK’s national security and make the country more resilient against potential aggression or sabotage, the government’s energy minister has said. Michael Shanks said widely dispersed wind farms and solar panels were much harder to target than large-scale fossil fuel power stations. They are also not vulnerable to supply shocks, such as the current o

stories.title: Children’s shoe retailers say closure of specialist shops is harming foot health
stories.summary: Experts report more young people with conditions such as bunions after wearing shoes that are too small or narrow Parents should care for their children’s feet in the same way as their eyes and teeth, according to footwear specialists who say they are seeing more young people with painful conditions such as bunions. Bunions are bony lumps on the side of the foot. People can be genetically pre-disposed but ill-fitting shoes are seen as an aggravating factor . Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump tells 60 Minutes he ‘wasn’t worried’ during correspondents’ dinner shooting
stories.summary: US president says in interview his curiosity probably slowed Secret Service efforts to rush him out of event Donald Trump spoke with CBS correspondent Norah O’Donnell in an interview that aired Sunday night on 60 Minutes describing his ordeal at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out. A gunman opened fire at the Washington Hilton hotel Saturday night, though he did not breach the basement-level ballroom where Trump was sitting at the time. The president described

stories.summary: A man and four children were able to escape the house at Bowen Mountain after fire broke out overnight. stories.title: Russia, North Korea agree 'long-term' military cooperation
stories.summary: Russia's defense minister and the chairman of the State Duma are both visiting North Korea. One helped inaugurate a memorial to North Korean soldiers killed fighting Ukraine, the other hashed out a new defense deal.

stories.title: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
stories.summary: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

stories.summary: Alex Greenwich says inquest crucial to understand which failures led to Bikram Lama’s death and to avoid similar deaths Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales attorney general, Michael Daley, has been urged to order an inquest into the death of Nepali rough sleeper Bikram Lama in Hyde Park. stories.title: Cheap spirits, pricey condoms: how war in the Middle East is shifting the way Australians spend
stories.summary: Beyond cutting back on driving, households are slicing deeper into their budgets, with some even forgoing healthcare Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast As soon as petrol prices started to rise in response to the Middle East conflict, many Australians – already grappling with high living costs – changed their spending habits. stories.title: Rival Labour factions understood to be discussing how to remove Keir Starmer as leader
stories.summary: Talk among MPs shifts from whether PM could be removed to possible processes for ‘transition’ as frustration grows Labour figures from across rival factions have begun circulating informal proposals for an “orderly transition” of power away from Keir Starmer, the Guardian understands. MPs have shifted discussions from speculating about whether the prime minister could be removed to how – including timelines, potential triggers and the mechanics of forcing a leadership contest. Continue reading..

stories.title: No headway in Middle East peace efforts as US and Iran refuse to yield
stories.summary: Deepening sense of deadlock despite regional diplomacy as Washington and Tehran show no signs of compromise Hopes of a breakthrough in negotiations between Iran and the US faded further on Sunday, amid a deepening sense of deadlock in the nearly two-month-long conflict despite intense regional diplomatic activity. Washington and Tehran appear unwilling to moderate rhetoric or make concessions, and there are no negotiations scheduled that might bring the war to a definitive end. Continue reading.

stories.title: London Marathon hails ‘greatest day’ as Sawe breaks two hours and records tumble
stories.summary: Men’s and women’s-only world records fall Organisers hope record number will finish by midnight The London Marathon’s organisers have hailed the “greatest day” in the event’s 45-year history after huge crowds watched Sabastian Sawe become the first man to shatter the two-hour barrier in an official race, and a world record tally of more than 60,000 runners started the event. By 6.30pm on Sunday evening, organisers were also hopeful of breaking the record number of 59,226 finishers, set by the Ne

stories.title: New IRA suspected over car explosion outside Belfast police station
stories.summary: Police say incident in Dunmurry in which no one was hurt shows ‘murderous intent still exists’ in paramilitaries “Murderous intent and capability” still exists within paramilitaries in Northern Ireland, officers have said after a car exploded outside a police station on the outskirts of Belfast. Detectives said they believed the New IRA was involved and are treating it as attempted murder. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK government move to delay social media ban faces pushback in Lords
stories.summary: Peers and campaigners say proposal for three-year window to impose controls breaks promise of quick action Peers will vote on Monday on a government move that could delay action on children’s access to social media for up to three years, which has triggered a backlash from campaigners and senior figures in the Lords. Ministers tabled an amendment to the children’s wellbeing and schools bill that would allow them to wait before introducing new restrictions, Critics warn it risks watering down ear

stories.title: UK urged to deploy EU-style ‘trade bazooka’ against Trump’s tariffs
stories.summary: ‘Inadequate economic security’ is putting growth and jobs at risk, says British Chambers of Commerce UK business leaders have called on the government to build an EU-style “trade bazooka” to protect Britain’s economic interests in response to the latest tariff threats from Donald Trump. As transatlantic tensions rise, the British Chambers of Commerce said the UK’s “inadequate economic security” was putting growth and jobs at risk. Continue reading...

stories.title: Michael moonwalks to $217m opening weekend, shattering box office records for a biopic
stories.summary: Michael Jackson biopic has shrugged off controversy, bad reviews and a troubled production to take $217m worldwide, including $97m in North America Michael , the big-budget Michael Jackson biopic, has shrugged off bad reviews and a troubled production to launch with a $97m opening in North American theaters, contributing to its enormous $217m (£160m, A$303m) worldwide box office and shattering the record for the biggest biopic opening of all time. The film, a highly authorised portrayal of the “

stories.title: Two bodies found after fire tears through home in Blue Mountains foothills
stories.summary: NSW police say human remains found and four people taken to hospital after overnight fire in Bowen Mountain house Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two bodies, believed to be those of children, have been found inside a Blue Mountains home destroyed by a fire which four other children and their father managed to escape. The family of six children, aged from three to 16, and their father, who is in his 30s, were insi

stories.title: Ryanair to shut Berlin base as it blames rise in German aviation tax
stories.summary: Trade union criticises airline’s plan to halve passenger numbers to the city as ‘purely profit-oriented’ Ryanair is to shut its Berlin operating base and cut its winter schedule to the German capital in half, blaming soaring aviation taxes in the country. The Irish budget carrier said its relocation of seven aircraft to other centres would reduce its Berlin passenger numbers from 4.5 million to 2.2 million a year, with flights in and out of the city served from October by planes based at other a

stories.title: Gunman was likely targeting Trump team, says official
stories.summary: The suspect in the attempted attack at the White House Correspondents' dinner is believed to have been targeting US President Donald Trump and members of his administration. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Colombia highway bomb attack kills at least 19
stories.summary: Suspected guerillas carried out a deadly explosives attack on the Pan-American Highway in southwest Colombia. The region has been gripped by violence over the past few days.

stories.title: SIPRI: Record arms spending, again
stories.summary: Global military spending hit a record high in 2025. The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) warns the trend is likely to continue — and carries additional risks.

stories.title: India news: Modi targets TMC in Bengal rally
stories.summary: PM Modi addressed rallies in West Bengal, taking jibes at the opposition party TMC ahead of the second phase of the state poll. Meanwhile, fresh clashes have erupted in Manipur.

stories.title: Mali: Fighting continues as Tuareg separatists claim Kidal
stories.summary: A spate of attacks was coordinated by Tuareg separatists and jihadis with links to al-Qaeda. Among them, a car bomb in Bamako killed Mali's defense minister.

stories.title: Germany news: Söder calls for compulsory military service
stories.summary: The Bavarian state premier has criticized the government's voluntary plan. Meanwhile, Chancellor Friedrich Merz condemned the shooting in Washington.

stories.title: Syria: First trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
stories.summary: A court in Damascus is trying one official in former President Bashar Assad's government in person, and several more in absentia. Assad and his brother are among the defendants. Both are thought to have fled to Russia.

stories.title: El-Sisi warns Middle East faces ‘attempts to redraw map’, calls for Gulf role in any Iran deal
stories.summary: The Egyptian president warned that the Middle East is entering a “critical phase”, with attempts to redraw its map, and called for state sovereignty to be respected.

stories.title: Iranian foreign minister heads to Moscow for meeting with Putin after second Pakistan trip
stories.summary: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi travelled once to Oman and twice to Pakistan over the weekend, before heading to Moscow to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin on Monday.

stories.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
stories.summary: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

stories.title: Trump and officials 'likely' targets of press dinner shooting suspect, authorities believe
stories.summary: The suspect is expected to be charged with assault of a federal officer and using a firearm during a crime of violence, officials have said.

stories.title: Trump evacuated after shots fired near White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Following the incident, US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the alleged suspect who reportedly opened fire near the ballroom where the annual correspondents' dinner was being held.

stories.title: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Security footage posted by President Donald Trump on social media appears to show the shooting suspect running toward White House Correspondents' dinner. Moments later, officials evacuated the area as gunshots were reported outside the ballroom.

stories.title: Who is Cole Allen? What we know about White House press dinner shooting suspect
stories.summary: The man arrested in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner, attended by US President Donald Trump, has been identified by US media as a mechanical engineer from California.

stories.title: Russian airstrikes kill at least seven people in Ukraine overnight
stories.summary: Dnipro bore the brunt of the attacks but Odesa and Kharkiv were also targeted in largest onslaught for several days Russian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said. Reports say that at least 34 people have been injured in the strikes, which lasted “practically all night”, according to the Dnipropetrovsk regional head, Oleksandr Hanzha. The bodies of four people were found in the r

stories.title: The tortoise and the hare: will China beat the US in the race back to the moon?
stories.summary: The rival superpowers are ramping up preparations for a crewed lunar landing nearly six decades after the first moon walk The world watched earlier this month as Nasa sent four astronauts around the moon – but to actually land on the surface the US is once again in a space race, this time with China. And China may well win. Both countries plan to build inhabited lunar bases – the first settlement on another celestial body – as well as searching for rare resources and using the deep space environ

stories.title: Patient allegedly attacks several nurses, police and member of the public at Sydney hospital
stories.summary: Calls for more security measures after man, 51, arrested over alleged assaults at Prince of Wales hospital Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A hospital attack that left a nurse seriously injured has fuelled calls for improved safety and security after several violent incidents in emergency departments. Police restrained a 51-year-old patient after he allegedly assaulted several nurses, police and a member of the pu

stories.title: Iran war: Peace talks on hold, what's next?
stories.summary: No direct talks took place between US and Iranian negotiators in Islamabad. A ceasefire in the Iran war continues to exist, but a roadmap to lasting peace remains elusive. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Queer as folk: The gay scandal that shook the German Empire
stories.summary: Germany's last emperor, a gilded porcelain vase and a general who had a heart attack in a pink tutu: The Eulenburg Affair shook the German Empire to its core and paved the way for the downfall of the monarchy.

stories.title: Sawe smashes 2-hour mark setting record at London Marathon
stories.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history in London as the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He beat the world record by more than a minute.

stories.title: Is Germany's pension system still secure?
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has sparked controversy by describing Germany's state pension as no more than "basic cover." What did he mean by that? And how does Germany compare internationally?

stories.title: I was in the room with Trump and heard the low thudding sound of gunfire
stories.summary: The BBC's Gary O'Donoghue describes the moment he and others dived for cover as shots rang out at the venue.

stories.title: Video shows correspondents' dinner suspect charge checkpoint
stories.summary: Surveillance video released by Donald Trump shows the suspect running past metal detectors as security agents draw their guns.

stories.title: Moment Trump rushed to safety as gunshots heard at White House Correspondents' Dinner
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump was quickly escorted to safety from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after gunshots were heard at the venue.

stories.title: World leaders react to White House press dinner shooting: no place for violence in democracy
stories.summary: Beyond condemning the attack, many leaders praised the US secret service, police and responders in ensuring the safety of guests. One officer was shot but was protected by a bulletproof vest and is in 'good condition', Trump said.

stories.title: On 40th Chernobyl disaster anniversary, Zelenskyy accuses Russia of committing 'nuclear terrorism'
stories.summary: Ukrainians gathered to honour those who died from the accident, as well as the hundreds of thousands who cleaned up after the disaster, exposing themselves to high levels of radiation.

stories.title: Debrecenben egy témáról szólt a kampány, és ez tökéletesen látszik a választási eredményben
stories.summary: A város a 2000-es években a Fidesz egyik legerősebb vidéki bástyája volt, 1998 óta folyamatosan fölényes győzelmeket arattak. Az elmúlt néhány évben a kormány már második fővárosként hivatkozott rá, amit a debreceniek azzal hálálták meg, hogy elsöprő többséggel választották meg a Tisza képviselőit.

stories.title: Hidegfront jön jövő héten, nagy szelekre is lehet számítani
stories.summary: A hosszú hétvégére viszont újra felmelegszik az idő.

stories.title: T. Danny lesz Curtis helyett a Megasztár egyik zsűritagja
stories.summary: Ez az első alkalom, hogy tehetségkutató tévéműsorba hívták meg zsűrizni.

stories.title: A Kutyapárt megszűnése is szerepel a forgatókönyvek között, a visszafizetendő 640 millió forint egyötödét tudták összeszedni
stories.summary: Eddig majdnem 120 millió forint gyűlt össze. Ha nem sikerül időben megszerezni a pénzt, a NAV a párt vezetőin, de még a jelölteken is követelheti az összeget. A párt innentől a működéséhez se kap állami támogatást, de erre is van válasz: vissza a gyökerekhez, évekig működtek így.

stories.title: Szándékosan ütötte el a biciklis futárt, a bíróság örökre eltiltotta első fokon a vezetéstől
stories.summary: A férfi többször is szemben hajtott be az egyirányú utcákba, emiatt szólt neki a futár.

stories.title: Tóth Gabi visszavonul a közösségi médiától, nem gondolta, hogy „mindenki a fideszesek vérét akarja inni”
stories.summary: Az énekesnő videójában arról is beszélt, hogy nagyon megviselte az elmúlt időszak, és szerinte „sokszor könnyebb ám az átlagos élet, mint a sztár élet”.

stories.title: Macron says EU’s mutual defence clause ‘not just words’
stories.summary: French president cites joint military aid to Cyprus as proof of Europe’s ability to defend itself during trip to Athens Emmanuel Macron has spoken up for Europe’s ability to defend itself, saying a mutual assistance clause, enshrined in the EU treaty, was unambiguous and “not just words”. The French president said the pact had already been proved in action when several member states sent military aid to Cyprus after a drone attack against a British airbase on the island on 28 February. Continue

stories.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
stories.summary: Al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claims responsibility for strikes on airport in capital, Bamako and four other cities Islamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali’s capital and other cities on Saturday in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years. The al-Qaida-linked militant group JNIM claimed responsibility for the attacks on Bamako’s international airport and four other cities in central and northern Mali on its website, Az-Zallaqa. It said the a

stories.title: US president cancels envoy trip to Pakistan for ceasefire talks – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed We have some images coming through the newswires of Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi , speaking with Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, and other officials in Islamabad this morning. Araghchi arrived in Pakistan last night. He wrote on social media that his trip would focus on “bilateral matters and regional developments”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.title: Tokyo workers encouraged to wear shorts to cut energy costs and keep cool
stories.summary: Officials hope more casual attire for public servants will save electricity during Iran war as summer heat approaches Public servants working for the Tokyo metropolitan government are being encouraged to swap their suits for shorts this summer to combat sweltering heat and rising energy costs caused by the US-Israel war on Iran . Inspired by Japan’s Cool Biz energy-saving initiative , Tokyo officials hope the measure will cut dependence on air conditioning. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pentagon email floats punishing NATO allies over Iran war
stories.summary: Washington has been considering sanctioning NATO allies over their non-involvement in the Iran war, with Spain in the spotlight after refusing the US use of its bases and airspace, according to reports.

stories.title: ‘Cries of delight’ as Sumatran orangutan filmed using canopy bridge to cross road for first time
stories.summary: After a two-year wait, video of a young male crossing above a road gives hope that critically endangered species can survive habitat fragmentation The critically endangered Sumatran orangutan has been filmed for the first time using a canopy bridge to cross a road. In 2024, conservationists in the Pakpak Bharat district of North Sumatra in Indonesia built the bridge high over the Lagan-Pagindar road, which provides an essential route for local people but which became a barrier for animals. Conti

stories.title: ‘Astonishing’ discovery could help save children from deadly disfiguring condition
stories.summary: A previously unknown species of bacteria found in patients with noma could be key to creating treatments for the neglected tropical disease The “astonishing” discovery of a new bacterium could open the door to better ways to prevent, detect and treat a fatal and disfiguring childhood disease, researchers hope. Noma, which is fatal in 90% of cases without treatment , begins as a sore on the gums but goes on to destroy the tissues of the mouth and face. Continue reading...

stories.title: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfires As wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods. As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition : anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s yearly salary. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump cancels his envoys’ Pakistan trip for Iran ceasefire negotiations
stories.summary: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were to travel to Islamabad to attempt to revive ceasefire negotiations Donald Trump said he has told US envoys not to go to Pakistan for more talks with Iran , shortly after Tehran’s top diplomat left Islamabad late on Saturday. Trump added to Fox News: “They can call us anytime they want.” The White House on Friday said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would travel to Pakistan’s capital to attempt to revive ceasefire negotiations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Jön a hidegfront és vele a viharos széllökések, de így is napos idő lesz vasárnap
stories.summary: A hőmérséklet csúcsértéke 16 és 21 fok körül alakul.

stories.title: Egymillió ember valósága fog most elolvadni
stories.summary: Az új kormány egyik első intézkedésének ígérte a közmédia beszántását és gyökeres megújítását. De hogyan fogják pszichésen feldolgozni az emberek, ha a tévé egyszer csak közli velük, hogy amit tíz éven át mondott nekik, az mind hazugság volt?

stories.title: Visszatért a Szerencseszombatba a Vitályos Eszternek odaszúró Freddie
stories.summary: Két hetes távollétéről nem esett szó, arról már igen, hogy a Fiestát a hazai zeneipar végletekig meghatározó szereplőjének tartja.

stories.title: Ilyen testvér a rémálmod
stories.summary: Heti streamingajánló a Szarvasbébi rendezőjének új sorozatával, egy animációs Stranger Things-utánlövéssel és egy elátkozott szigeten játszódó krimivel.

stories.title: A fideszes Bánki Erik sem veszi fel a mandátumát
stories.summary: Posztjában azt írta, 36 év után befejezi a frontpolitikát, de a háttérből mindent megtesz azért, hogy a „közösségünk egyben maradjon, erősödjön, és mielőbb vissza tudjunk térni a kormányzásba.”

stories.title: Elképesztő rekord a londoni maratonon, először futották le versenyen két órán belül a távot
stories.summary: A kenyai Sabastian Sawe 1:59:30-as időt futott, ami a világ eddigi leggyorsabb maratoni ideje.

stories.title: Minden lassan, szinte észrevétlenül csúszik el
stories.summary: A Hajnalcsillag sorozat negyedik kötetének egyik legnyugtalanítóbb vonása a bizonytalanság: nincs egyértelmű határ jó és rossz, valóság és képzelet, döntés és sodródás között. Kritika Karl Ove Knausgård új könyvéről.

stories.title: Elefánt ölt meg egy antilopokra vadászó amerikai milliomost
stories.summary: A New York Timesnak nyilatkozó ismerőse szerint Ernie Dosionak tele volt a szobája kitömött állatokkal, a kockázatokkal is tisztában volt.

stories.title: Romlottak a kilátások az amerikai-iráni tárgyalásokban, miután Trump lefújta Witkoffék pakisztáni útját
stories.summary: Pakisztáni kormányzati források szerint az amerikai erők elszállították a biztonsági berendezéseket Iszlámábádból, ami arra utal, hogy az amerikai delegáció nem egyhamar nem tér vissza.

stories.title: Az igazságügyi miniszter szerint a Trump-kormány tagjait akarta célba venni a Washington Hiltonban lövöldöző férfi
stories.summary: Az eddigi információk szerint Cole Thomas Allen egyedül volt.

stories.title: „Szabadságot mindenkinek” – így indult a Fidesz 1989-ben egy archív BBC-felvétel szerint
stories.summary: A kommunista rezsim elnyomását is bemutató dokumentumfilmben megjelenik a fiatal Szájer József is.

stories.title: Donald Trump a szombati lövöldözésről: ezért van szükség a bálteremre
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök arra jutott, hogy nem történt volna meg a lövöldözés, ha kész a szuper védett bálterem.

stories.title: A brit miniszterelnök és Trump a Hormuzi-szoros sürgős újranyitásáról tárgyaltak
stories.summary: Közben ezen a hétvégén sem jött el a diplomáciai áttörés az iráni háborúban.

stories.title: A lucfenyők hazai pusztulásán látszik igazán a klímaváltozás súlyos hatása
stories.summary: 269 településről jeleztek pusztulást.

stories.title: Mali: Explosions, gunfire rock main military camp near capital
stories.summary: Mali's army said unidentified "terrorist" groups had attacked several positions in the capital and elsewhere in the country.

stories.title: Tunisia suspends rights group amid widening repression
stories.summary: Tunisian authorities have ordered one of Africa's oldest rights groups to cease its activities for a month. The move comes as critics say the country is moving toward authoritarian rule.

stories.title: Zelenskyy ready to hold Ukraine-Russia talks in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country is ready to hold talks with Russia in Azerbaijan. Meanwhile, Russian attacks killed several people across Ukraine.

stories.title: UK-US ties tested: Britain courts Trump with King Charles
stories.summary: On a state visit to the US, the British monarch is being deployed to help steady increasingly strained US-UK relations. Can King Charles help repair the damaged so-called "special relationship?"

stories.title: Mexico says alleged CIA agents 'not authorized' to operate on its soil
stories.summary: Two US citizens believed to be CIA agents were killed in a car accident following an anti-drug trafficking operation. Mexico says they entered the country as a tourist and a diplomat.

stories.title: Palestinians vote in municipal elections in Gaza, West Bank
stories.summary: Palestinians headed to the polls in the occupied West Bank and a part of Gaza in a first vote since the Gaza war.

stories.title: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today
stories.summary: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump cancels envoys' trip to Islamabad
stories.summary: Donald Trump said that he didn't want US officials to "sit around talking about nothing" in Islamabad. It comes after Iran said that there would be no direct talks with the US. More on DW.

stories.title: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
stories.summary: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

stories.title: Trump cancels US negotiators' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks as Araghchi departs Islamabad
stories.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were expected to arrive in Pakistan on Saturday to conduct a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran.

stories.title: West Bank and part of Gaza vote in first municipal elections since war
stories.summary: Municipal elections in the West Bank and central Gaza mark the first vote since the Gaza war, with Fatah-linked lists dominant and Hamas absent from the race.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says he is ready to meet Putin in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: In a significant first visit to the South Caucasus since the start of Russia’s all-out war, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in a statement next to President Ilham Aliyev he is ready to meet Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan after the US-led diplomatic talks have stalled over the past weeks.

stories.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

stories.title: Russian strikes kill at least seven and injure dozens across Ukraine
stories.summary: Most of those killed and injured in Russian overnight attacks were in the eastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, where an attack killed at least five and injured more than 30.

stories.title: Hope in Gaza as 300 couples get married at one time
stories.summary: The 300 couples were selected in a draw of nearly 2,000 people to be a part of the joint celebration.

stories.title: Palestinians vote in West Bank and central Gaza municipal elections, the first since war started
stories.summary: The elections, held in the Israeli-occupied West Bank and in Gaza's Deir al-Balah, are taking place against the backdrop of a highly volatile political and security environment.

stories.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.title: Rights groups critical as Venezuela prisoner release scheme 'coming to an end'
stories.summary: More than 500 political prisoners are thought to still be in jail, despite the releases since the amnesty law was brought in.

stories.title: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.title: Watch: How one orangutan braved new bridge to unite his split community
stories.summary: The forest where the Sumatran orangutans live has been split by a road.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
stories.summary: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian strikes in Dnipro, blackouts in Karachi and Manchester City’s Erling Haaland – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
stories.summary: Small boat destroyed in video posted on social media as US campaign has killed at least 178 people since September The US military announced on Friday that it killed two people in an attack on a boat in the eastern Pacific, part of a series of deadly strikes on vessels in recent months which it claims are targeting “narco-trafficking” operations. The US Southern Command declared in a social media post on X that Gen Francis L Donovan directed Joint Task Force Southern Spear, the counter-narcotics

stories.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
stories.summary: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

stories.title: Why investment in Caspian transit routes is crucial for energy security
stories.summary: As old energy routes buckle under the pressure of war and geopolitical disruption, governments and companies are converging on the Caspian region as the corridor that could keep Eurasia's energy flowing.

stories.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.title: US to allow firing squads, gas and electrocution for federal executions
stories.summary: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

stories.title: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
stories.summary: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

stories.title: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
stories.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

stories.title: Audiencián fogadta XIV. Leó a Tisza képviselőit
stories.summary: „Az elkövetkező munkánkat innentől a pápa áldásával is végezzük” – írta Kollár Kinga.

stories.title: Palácsik-Ráthonyi Tímea: Sokan próbáltak kiforgatni az örökségemből
stories.summary: A néhai Andy Vajna egykori felesége Friderikusz Sándor talkshow-jában azt mondta, az örökséggel együtt megtapasztalta az emberek mély kapzsiságát.

stories.title: Ice block stalls hundreds of Everest climbers at base camp
stories.summary: Officials assessing route after serac between base camp and camp one deemed unstable and too risky for climbers A large ice block on the route just above the Mount Everest base camp has forced hundreds of climbers and local guides to delay their attempt to scale the world’s highest peak. The serac between base camp and camp one was unstable and risky for climbers, said Himal Gautam of Nepal’s department of mountaineering on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Britain should seek to rejoin EU, says civil servant who led Brexit department
stories.summary: Philip Rycroft says promises on issues from economics to immigration have not lived up to expectations Britain should start talking about rejoining the EU, according to a former senior civil servant who ran the Brexit department. Philip Rycroft, who was permanent secretary of the Department for Exiting the EU, said the “argument was there to be won” about going back into Europe, adding that a “clear-headed appraisal of what is in the country’s best interests” was needed. However, he said rejoini

stories.title: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
stories.summary: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas markets At the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel. China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage . Now they are turning their attention to what they are b

stories.title: UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo
stories.summary: Internal email proposes US should reassess support for UK claim to islands because of lack of support for Iran war UK politics live – latest updates Downing Street has been forced to insist that Britain will not yield sovereignty over the Falkland Islands, after a leaked Pentagon email proposed the US should reassess its support for the UK’s claim on the islands because of a lack of British support over Iran. The memo reflected ways in which the Trump administration could punish Britain for fail

stories.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
stories.summary: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

stories.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind
stories.summary: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

stories.title: The silent power of memes
stories.summary: They are funny, provocative and sometimes tasteless. But memes are also powerful political tool, shaping our perception — usually without us noticing.

stories.title: Is the far-right AfD misusing its parliamentary questions?
stories.summary: The Alternative for Germany (AfD) has submitted thousands of official questions in state parliaments — a right of any elected party. But critics argue that their focus is meant to pressure political opponents.

stories.title: Ukraine: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
stories.summary: Russian President Putin "may or not may not" attend the G20 in Miami in December, says the Kremlin. Meanwhile, after securing an EU loan, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy continued his diplomatic offensive in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended by three weeks, Trump says
stories.summary: Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, which was set to expire on Monday, by three weeks. Meanwhile, the US and Iran persist in its tense standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war sparks push to transform Syria into global energy corridor
stories.summary: Washington is reportedly exploring a plan to turn Syria into a key energy corridor, linking their pipelines to Turkey and Europe.

stories.title: Over 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN Women report estimates
stories.summary: From becoming the head of a household and facing challenges linked to housing and land rights, to restricted access to essential healthcare and reproductive services, how are women disproportionately affected by war?

stories.title: Armenia marks 111th anniversary of 1915 massacres in Yerevan
stories.summary: Armenia marked the 111th anniversary of the 1915 massacres on 23 and 24 April 2026 with marches and ceremonies in Yerevan, drawing thousands of participants.

stories.title: 20,000 teddy bears in Washington highlight abducted Ukrainian children
stories.summary: A sea of 20,000 teddy bears on the US National Mall highlights Ukrainian children abducted since 2021, urging action as the war’s toll on families grows.

stories.title: Iran 'trapped in slogans' as nuclear talks with US stall after naval standoff, former diplomat says
stories.summary: Former Iranian diplomat Kourosh Ahmadi has warned that Tehran and Washington have found themselves stuck in their individual and contrasting narratives, risking nuclear talks as tensions over blockades and enrichment grow.

stories.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.title: Ukraine’s Chernobyl ‘liquidators’ return 40 years after disaster
stories.summary: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the 1986 Chernobyl disaster in April 2026 as former clean-up workers returned to the site.

stories.title: US soldier charged with using Maduro raid intel to win €341,000 on Polymarket
stories.summary: Polymarket said in a statement it had flagged the user who made the bets to the US Department of Justice and cooperated with the investigation.

stories.title: Indonesian peacekeeper dies of wounds suffered in Lebanon last month, UNIFIL says
stories.summary: His death brings the number of peacekeepers killed since the start of the most recent war between Israel and Hezbollah on 2 March to six.

stories.title: US envoys Witkoff, Kushner to head to Pakistan for Iran talks on Saturday, White House says
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Pakistan on Friday night, but his ministry has ruled out direct talks with the US delegation.

stories.title: Powerful tornado tears across Oklahoma, damaging homes and blocking roads
stories.summary: A powerful tornado struck Oklahoma damaging homes, downing power lines, and blocking roads. The storm hit near Enid and surrounding areas, prompting emergency responses. No fatalities were reported, with only minor injuries confirmed so far.

stories.title: Behind Nigeria's murky coup plot - the money, the prayers and a Nollywood arrest
stories.summary: What court papers and sources close to the investigation have told the BBC about the scope of the plan.

stories.title: Prince Harry visits Kyiv to back Ukraine and spotlight mine clearance
stories.summary: Ukraine welcomed Prince Harry to Kyiv on Thursday 23 April 2026 for a two day visit that remained unannounced until his arrival.

stories.title: Mandelson under formal investigation by EU's anti-fraud office
stories.summary: The EU's anti-fraud office confirms it has "sufficient information" to launch an investigation, but does not say if it involves fraud.

stories.title: Israeli police investigate after officers 'cut Palestinian flag' from skullcap
stories.summary: The British Israeli man says he was detained after someone took offence at his kippah embroidered with Israeli and Palestinian flags.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.summary: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
stories.summary: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

stories.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
stories.summary: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

stories.title: China car giant BYD says it can thrive without US
stories.summary: With the price of fuel rising China's BYD says it is positioning itself to benefit from the global shift away from fossil fuels.

stories.title: Italy tests R1 robotic museum guide in Turin’s historic Palazzo Madama
stories.summary: Italy is trialling the R1 robot guide at Turin’s Palazzo Madama to ease crowding and improve access, explaining centuries-old artworks to visitors.

stories.title: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.title: A nation built on pan-African principles faces questions about racism
stories.summary: Several black Zambians tell the BBC they sometimes feel like they are second-class citizens in their own country.

stories.title: Meet the men calling out 'masculinity' trends
stories.summary: Influencers promoting looksmaxxing and narrow male beauty standards are being called out by a new wave of men on social media.

stories.title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
stories.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

stories.title: Heated Rivalry, The Housemaid and Fourth Wing: TikTok launches BookTok bestseller list
stories.summary: The inaugural top 20 is entirely made up of female authors, with Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently.

stories.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical state election, as government seeks to ‘purify’ electoral roll
stories.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from the electoral roll ahead of the West Bengal elections this week Millions of people in the Indian state of West Bengal have been stripped of their vote ahead of a critical state election this week, after a controversial electoral revision described by critics as a “bloodless political genocide” and mass disenfranchisement of minorities. In West Bengal, a total of 9.1 million names have been deleted from the registe

stories.title: The international right has CPAC. Has the left finally found its answer? | Owen Jones
stories.summary: Spain’s PM, Pedro Sánchez, hosted the inaugural meeting of the Global Progressive Mobilisation. Keir Starmer and other social democrats were notable by their absence Can progressives push back the rising tide of authoritarianism? Thousands of people gathered in Barcelona this weekend in search of an answer. The occasion was the inaugural Global Progressive Mobilisation – an ambitious initiative backed by the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez – which drew an impressive cast: Brazil’s Lula, Me
